1.1: The Song of Songs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel, which is Song of Songs, and called in Hebrew, Song of Songs
1.2: He shall kiss me from the kisses of his mouth, for your affections are better than wine
1.3: For fragrance of your sweet ointments your name is fine oil of Myrrh, because of this the girls love you
1.4: Pull me after you, let us run. The King brought me to his bedroom. We will rejoice and we will leap for joy with you. Your love will be remembered more than wine, and your affection more than the upright
1.5: I am black and beautiful, daughters of Jerusalem, like the tents of Qedar and like the curtains of Solomon
1.6: You shall not look at me, because I am black, because the sun has darkened me. The children of my mother fought hard with me; they made me keeper of vineyards, and my vineyard I have not kept
1.7: Show me him whom my soul has loved. How have you fed and how have you dined at noonday?, lest I shall be as a stray in the flock of your sheep
1.8: But you shall know yourself; you are beautiful among women. Come into the footprints of the sheep and feed your kids in the tents of Shepherds
1.9: I have compared you to a mare in the chariot of Pharaoh, my kinswoman
1.10: Your cheeks are beautiful with braids and your neck with necklaces
1.11: We shall make chains of gold for you with studs of silver
1.12: With the King on his seat my spikenard gives its fragrance
1.13: A pouch of myrrh is he who loves me; between my breasts he will stay
1.14: Bouquets of henna flowers is my beloved in a vineyard of Ain Gad
1.15: Behold, you are beautiful, my kinswoman, behold, you are beautiful! Your eyes are of doves!
1.16: Behold, you are beautiful, my love, also you are sweet, and our bed is canopied
1.17: The beams of our house are cedars, the joists of cypress

2.1: Like the lily of Sharon, the lily of the valley
2.2: Like lilies among thorns, so is my intimate woman among the daughters
2.3: As apples in the trees of the forest, so is my love among the sons; for his shadow I lusted and I sat; his fruit is sweet to my palate
2.4: He brought me to the house of wine; he appointed love for me
2.5: Sustain me with delicacies, surround me with fruit, for I am love sick
2.6: His left hand is under my head and his right will embrace me
2.7: Swear, oh daughters of Jerusalem, by the deer or by the fawns of the field, you will not wake up and you will not wake up the beloved until she pleases
2.8: The voice of my love: behold he comes skipping on the mountains, leaping on the hills
2.9: My love is like a deer or to the fawn of a hart on the mountains of Bayth Eil, behold he is standing behind our wall; he watches from the windows; he stoops at the thresholds
2.10: My beloved answered and he said to me: "arise, for you, my intimate one, you are beautiful, and go
2.11: For , behold, the winter has passed and is gone!
2.12: The blossoms have appeared in the earth. The time of pruning has arrived! The voice of turtle-doves is heard in our country
2.13: The fig tree has given its figs and the vines and the vine buds give a fragrance. Arise! You are beautiful my intimate one and come!
2.14: My dove in the clefts of the rock, in secret places of a hedge, show me your beauty, your appearance, and make me hear your voice, for your voice is sweet and your appearance is beautiful!
2.15: Catch for us foxes, small foxes ruining the vineyards and our vines of vine buds
2.16: My beloved is mine and I am his, who feeds among the lilies
2.17: While the day will grow cold and the shadows shall lengthen, return, my love, be like a deer or to a fawn of a hart on the mountains of spices

3.1: On my bed in the nights I begged him whom my soul has loved; I sought him and I did not find him. I called him and I did not find him
3.2: I shall arise now and I shall go around in the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I shall seek him whom my soul has loved. I sought him and I did not find him
3.3: The guards found me who walk around in the city: "Have you seen him whom my soul loves?"
3.4: When I passed from them a little until I found him whom my soul loved, I seized him and I did not let go of him until I brought him to the house of my mother to the bedroom of her who bore me
3.5: I charge you daughters of Jerusalem by the deer or by the stags of the field, do not rise and wake up love until she pleases
3.6: Who is this who comes up from the wilderness like a fume of smoke? He is sweet with myrrh and with frankincense from every powder of sweet spices!
3.7: Behold the bed of Solomon! Sixty mighty men surround it of the mighty men of Israel
3.8: All of them hold a sword and are teachers of war, each man’s sword on his thigh from fear of the night
3.9: King Solomon made himself a tower from wood of Lebanon
3.10: Its pillars he made of silver, its floor of gold and a covering of purple; inside it is paved with love from the daughters of Jerusalem
3.11: Go forth and see, daughters of Zion, King Solomon with the crown with which his mother crowned him in the day of his nuptials in the day of the joy of his heart

4.1: Behold, you are beautiful my intimate one, behold, you are beautiful! Your eyes are of a dove; outside of your silence, your hair is like a flock of goats that goes up from Mount Gelad
4.2: Your teeth are like a flock that is shorn going up from their washing, all bearing twins and none is barren among them
4.3: Your lips like a cord of scarlet and your speech beautiful as the blossoms of a pomegranate
4.4: Your neck, in your stillness, is like the tower of David; your throat built with belts, one thousand shields hanging upon it, all quivers of mighty men
4.5: Your two breasts as two gazelles, twins of deer that feed among the lilies
4.6: While the day will grow cool and the shadows of evening will decline, I shall go to the mountains of myrrh and to the hills of frankincense
4.7: You are entirely beautiful, my intimate one, and there is no defect in you
4.8: Come from Lebanon, my sister bride, come from Lebanon! Come and cross over from the top of Amana and from the top of Sanir and Khermon, from the den of lions and from the mountains of leopards
4.9: You have given me heart, my sister bride, you have given me heart with one of your eyes, with one necklace of your neck
4.10: How beautiful are your breasts, my sister bride! How much better are your breasts than wine, and the fragrance of your sweet spices than all sweet spices!
4.11: Your lips drip honeycomb! All honey and milk are under your lips and the fragrance of your vesture is as the fragrance of Lebanon!
4.12: A garden that is enclosed is my sister bride! A garden that is enclosed, a spring that is sealed
4.13: Your nudity is a paradise of pomegranates with the fruit of produce; henna flower with spikenard
4.14: Spikenard and saffron, crocus, cane and cinnamon with trees of frankincense, myrrh and aloes, with all the best of sweet spices
4.15: A spring garden, a well of living waters that flows from Lebanon
4.16: Awaken, oh North, and come south wind! Blow in my garden! My sweet fragrances shall flow, and my beloved shall come to his garden, and he will eat from the fruit of his produce

5.1: I have come to my garden, my sister bride; I have come to my garden. I have picked my myrrh with my sweet spices. I ate my sweetness with my honey. I drank my wine with my milk. Eat, my friends, and drink and be drunk, my beloved
5.2: I was sleeping and my heart was awake. The voice of my love who was knocking: "Open to me, my sister, my intimate, my innocent dove, for my head is filled with dew and my curls from drops of the night
5.3: I took off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I get them dirty?
5.4: My beloved reached his hand from the opening and my bowels stirred for him
5.5: I stood that I would open to my beloved, and my hand dripped myrrh and my fingers dripped myrrh on the handle of the bolt
5.6: I opened to my beloved and my beloved had declined, he passed on; my soul went out at his speaking. I searched for him and I did not find him; I called him and he did not answer me
5.7: The guards who walk around in the city found me; they struck me and they beat me; the keepers of the wall took my bridal veil from me
5.8: I charge you daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, tell him that I am sick with love
5.9: Who is your beloved more than a beloved, beautiful among women? Who is your beloved more than a beloved, beautiful among women? Who is your beloved more than a beloved, that thus you have charged us?
5.10: My beloved is white and blood red, and choice of ten thousand
5.11: His head is like rock of gold, his locks are straight and black as ravens
5.12: His eyes are like those of doves by pools of waters that are washed in milk and sit with harmony
5.13: His cheeks are as beds of sweet spices; his lips like the lilies that drip myrrh and spikenard
5.14: His hands are like a fortress city of gold that is full of stones of gold; his belly the work of ivory on the blowing of sapphire
5.15: His legs are pillars of marble which were set in order on sockets of gold; his chest is like Lebanon and choice as cedars
5.16: His palate is like honeycombs that are sweet and his garments are desirable; this is my beloved and this is my intimate friend, daughters of Jerusalem!

6.1: Where has your beloved gone, beautiful one among women? Where has your beloved turned, that we may seek him with you?
6.2: My beloved went down to his garden and to the garden beds of sweet spices, that he may feed in the gardens and he may gather the lilies
6.3: I am my beloved’s and my beloved is mine, who feeds among the lilies
6.4: You are beautiful, my intimate one, as desire; you are beautiful as Jerusalem, and you are awesome as the chosen one
6.5: Turn your eyes from me, for they make me flutter; you hair is like a flock of goats going up from the Mountain of Gelad
6.6: Your teeth are like a flock that is shorn going up from washing, all of which are bearing twins, and none is barren among them
6.7: Your neck out of your silence is like blossoms of pomegranate
6.8: Sixty are Queens and eighty are Concubines and the young girls there have no number
6.9: One is my innocent dove; one she is to her mother, and chosen she is to her who bore her. The daughters saw her and praised her, and the Queens and Concubines, and they praised her
6.10: Who is she, this one who looks forth like the morning, beautiful like the moon, pure like the sun, awesome like a Princess?
6.11: I have come down to the garden of walnut trees that I may see the fruit of the valley, to see if the vine has borne fruit and if the pomegranates have budded
6.12: I had not known; my soul set me in a chariot of the people that was prepared
6.13: Return! Return, Shilumite! Return, return, and we shall gaze at you! What will you see in the Shilumite who comes down as rejoicing, like the rejoicing of army camps?

7.1: How beautiful are your feet in sandals, daughter of preparation, and the form of your thighs as a necklace, the work of the hands of a Craftsman!
7.2: Your navel is a goblet that is round that lacks no mixture in it. Your belly is a heap of wheat that is enclosed in lilies
7.3: Your two breasts are as two fawns, twins of deer that feed in the lilies
7.4: Your neck is like a tower of ivory. Your eyes as the pools of Kheshbon at the gate of the palace of the great ones. Your nose is like the tower of Lebanon that gazes at the face of Darmsuq
7.5: Your head upon you is like Karmela and the tresses of your head like the purple garment of the King that is tied in rows
7.6: How beautiful you are! How sweet you are, oh beloved daughter of delights!
7.7: And this your stature is like a palm tree and your breasts are like grape clusters!
7.8: I said:"I shall go up into the palm tree and I shall seize on its branches, and your breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the fragrance of your face is like apples
7.9: And your palate is like fine wine that goes to my beloved for uprightness that shakes my lips and my teeth
7.10: I am my beloved's and his inclination is toward me
7.11: Come, my beloved, let us go out to the field! We will stay in a village!
7.12: And we shall go early into the vineyard; we will see if the vine has borne fruit and the buds have come out and the pomegranates have budded; there I shall give you my breasts
7.13: The mandrakes have given a fragrance, and all our fruits, new ones and old ones, my beloved, I have kept for you upon our gates

8.1: Who shall give you as a brother to me? My breasts nursed my lambs. I will find you in the street. I will kiss you and they will not despise me
8.2: I shall lead you and I shall bring you to the house of my mother and to the bedroom of her who bore me. I shall give you drink from my sweet wine and from the sweetness of my pomegranates
8.3: His left hand is under my head and his right hand will embrace me
8.4: I charge you, daughters of Jerusalem, do not wake up and do not wake up love until she pleases!
8.5: Who is this coming up from the wilderness and supported by her beloved under the apple tree? I awakened you and there your mother was in labor with you; there your mother labored with you!
8.6: Set me as the seal on your heart and as a seal on your arm, because love is as strong as death, and jealousy hard as Sheol! Its blazing is like the blazing of fire and the flame!
8.7: Many waters are not able to quench love, and rivers do not overwhelm it! If a man will give all the riches of his house for love, mocking they will make a mockery of him
8.8: We have a little sister and she has no breasts; what shall we do for our sister in the day in which she is spoken for?
8.9: If she is a wall, we will build up on her battlements of silver, and if she is a door, we will bind upon her a title of cedar
8.10: I am a wall and my breasts are towers; then I became in his eyes as she who found peace
8.11: There was a vineyard for Solomon and its fruit was abundant. I have given a vineyard to the keepers; a man gave a thousand pieces of silver for its fruits
8.12: My vineyard is mine before me. A thousand coins for you, Solomon, and two hundred to the keepers of the fruit
8.13: Make me hear those dwelling in the gardens and they listen to your voice!
8.14: Return my beloved! Be like a roe or a fawn of a hart on the mountains of spices!
